value:
Hi there, thanks for reaching out! Setting up a default page in your Azure WebApp is straightforward—you'll want to make sure your default document (like index.html) is named correctly and placed in the root directory of your site. Azure typically looks for files like index.html, default.html, or default.htm in order of priority.

A few things to check: First, verify that your HTML file is actually in the root folder (not in a subfolder) where you uploaded it via FTP. Second, check your WebApp's application settings to confirm the default document is configured—you can do this in the Azure portal under your WebApp's Configuration settings. If you've already set a default document there and it's still not working, let me know what error you're seeing or what happens when you visit your site, and I'll help troubleshoot further.
